title: Chocolate Beetroot Cake Recipe
summary: This Chocolate Beetroot Cake recipe combines the earthy sweetness of beetroot with the rich, deep flavours of dark chocolate, resulting in a moist and decadent dessert that’s both surprising and delightful. The beetroot not only adds a unique flavour but also contributes to the cake's incredibly tender texture, making it a perfect choice for those who love a good chocolate cake with a twist. Whether you're serving it as a show-stopping dessert at a birthday party or simply enjoying it as a treat with afternoon tea, this beetroot cake will impress with its rich chocolatey goodness. The addition of beetroot also makes the cake naturally moist, ensuring it stays fresh longer. Pair this with a dollop of coconut cream or a sprinkle of cocoa powder for an extra layer of indulgence. Perfect for lovers of traditional cakes looking to try something new!

method.description: Heat the oven to 160°C. Grease a 23cm springform cake tin with a little butter, then line the base with baking parchment.
Drain the beetroot, roughly chop it, then blitz to a thick purée in a blender or food processor. Set aside.

Roughly chop the dark chocolate for the cake, then transfer to a heatproof bowl with the butter. Melt together over a pan of barely simmering water (or in a microwave, zapping on high in 30 second intervals then stirring) until melted.

Measure the flour, cocoa powder, sugar, salt and bicarb into a large bowl, then whisk together. Crack in the eggs, add the beetroot purée, melted chocolate and butter, then whisk everything together until a smooth, rich chocolate cake batter forms.
Scrape the batter into your lined tin. Bake in the centre of the oven for 40-45 mins until risen, coming away from the edges and a skewer inserted into the centre comes out clean.
Leave the cake to cool completely in the tin – this can even be done the night before and kept well wrapped.

Ganache time. Finely chop the chocolate for the ganache and put into a heatproof bowl. Cut the butter into small cubes, then add to the chocolate bowl. Heat the cream in a small saucepan or microwave until it’s just steaming, then pour evenly all over the chocolate and butter. Leave to stand for 1 min, then stir with a spatula until you have a smooth and glossy ganache.

Put the beetroot chocolate cake onto a serving plate then pour the ganache all over the top. Eat now while the ganache is slightly warm with some extra cold cream, or wait for it to set a little before tucking in. Either way is a win.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
